summary: A single fourslash test assertion failed: the code-fix for 'codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts' produced the label 'Add return type \'Foo<string>\'' instead of the expected 'Add return type \'Foo<string, string[…\'''. This caused the test run to exit with code 1.
root_cause: The fourslash test 'codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts' failed because the code-fix implementation returned a simplified generic type 'Foo<string>' instead of the expected fuller generic type 'Foo<string, string[…]'. The assertion in verifyCodeFix detected the mismatch.
relevant_tests: fourslash tests > tests/cases/fourslash/codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts > fourslash test codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts runs correctly

evidence.quote: AssertionError: expected 'Add return type \'Foo<string>\'' to equal 'Add return type \'Foo<string, string[…'
evidence.reason: This is the exact assertion failure: the actual code-fix description uses an oversimplified generic type 'Foo<string>' while the test expects a more complete type 'Foo<string, string[…]'.
The failure originates in the verifyCodeFix method, confirming this is a code-fix behavior mismatch caught by the test harness.
Only one test failed out of 106366 passing, isolating the root cause to this specific fourslash test.
This is the specific test file that failed, related to generic type annotation oversimplification in the missing-type-annotation code fix.

suggested_fix: Update the code-fix logic that generates the 'Add return type' description to preserve all type parameters of generic types (e.g., 'Foo<string, string[]>') instead of oversimplifying to 'Foo<string>', or update the test expectation in 'tests/cases/fourslash/codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts' if the simplified output is intentional.
